items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Sentry Flaw (CVE-2026-10520) Exploited Within 24 Hours of PoC Release
items.summary: CVE-2026-10520 is a CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated OS command injection flaw in Ivanti Sentry that lets remote attackers run commands as root. Shadowserver confirmed internet-exposed Sentry gateways were already backdoored within 24 hours of public PoC code dropping; CISA added it to the KEV catalog with a federal patch mandate.
items.why: Root-level compromise of Sentry is equivalent to unrestricted access to every mailbox, calendar, and app the gateway brokers, and the exploitation window between PoC release and active compromise was under a day.

items.title: Operation Endgame Dismantles SocGholish/Evil Corp Malware Infrastructure
items.summary: The FBI, Europol, NHTCU (Netherlands), BKA (Germany), and RCMP (Canada) jointly seized 106 servers and 101 domains tied to SocGholish, the fake-browser-update malware framework linked to Russia's Evil Corp, remediating nearly 15,000 infected websites worldwide.
items.why: SocGholish has been the top malware downloader globally (around 60% of all downloader infections) since 2017 and a key initial-access broker for ransomware gangs, making this one of the largest Operation Endgame disruptions to date.

items.title: usbliter8 BootROM Exploit Chain Defeats Pointer Authentication on Apple A12/A13 Silicon
items.summary: Security firm Paradigm Shift disclosed usbliter8, an unpatchable BootROM exploit chaining a hardware bug in the Synopsys DWC2 USB controller with a firmware flaw to fully compromise the boot chain on Apple A12/A13 and S4/S5 SoCs. On A13 devices, which add Pointer Authentication Codes, researchers built a multi-stage heap-corruption and interrupt-handler attack to defeat PAC.
items.why: It is a genuinely novel hardware/firmware attack class that defeats a modern memory-safety mitigation (PAC) and can never be patched in software on affected silicon, with real implications for forensic device-unlock scenarios.

items.title: Werewolf Threat Actors Abuse Microsoft's Fondue.exe to Side-Load Malware
items.summary: Threat clusters tracked as Paper Werewolf, Versatile Werewolf, and the newly identified Eagle Werewolf are abusing the legitimate Windows 'Features on Demand' binary Fondue.exe to side-load a malicious appwiz.cpl, using fake Starlink registration and drone-pilot-training lures against government, military, and drone-engineering targets.​
items.why: It's a living-off-the-land technique abusing a binary nobody monitors, paired with AI-accelerated tooling, and is actively targeting defense and critical-infrastructure personnel.

items.title: GreatXML: Researcher Releases Working BitLocker Bypass via Recovery Partition XML Files
items.summary: Researcher 'Chaotic Eclipse' published GreatXML, a working exploit that drops malicious unattend.xml and ReAgent.xml files onto a Windows recovery partition to spawn a SYSTEM-privileged shell with full access to the BitLocker-encrypted volume after booting into Windows Recovery Environment.
items.why: It's a fully encryption-bypassing technique against a default-on Windows protection, released with working PoC, part of an ongoing wave of disclosures (BlueHammer, UnDefend, RedSun, RoguePlanet) from the same researcher targeting Microsoft.
